title: Catalogue globalization test suite
description: Dedicated tests for migration integrity, global dedupe correctness, the same-item double-tracking edge case, price-history aggregation across projects, item-override precedence, and suggestion-approval reuse of the global identity key, plus full regression of the existing catalogue/scoring/matching/web suites.
acceptance_criteria: Migration integrity test runs against a sanitised copy of the real data/product_finder.db, asserts zero data loss via row-count reconciliation, and asserts scoring/matching output for a fixed set of known listings is unchanged before/after

Global dedupe test: two items independently create the same product by identity key; after the dedupe pass, exactly one products row exists and both items retain independent item_products rows with their own match_terms/target_deal_price
Same-item double-tracking edge case test: an item with two pre-existing in-item duplicate products migrates to exactly one item_products row for that item

Price-history aggregation test: two projects' matched listings for the same global product combine into one shared typical_used_price median, using a fixture where each project alone would compute a different median
Item-override precedence test: an item with a target_deal_price override scores differently from an item with no override tracking the same global product
Suggestion-approval test: approving a suggestion for an already-globally-known manufacturer/model attaches to the existing product rather than creating a duplicate
Full existing test suite (test_catalogue.py, test_scoring.py, test_web.py, etc.) passes with no behavioural regression for a single-project database
notes: See docs/adr/0007-catalogue-globalization.md, 'Acceptance criteria' and 'Tests needed' sections — this feature operationalises both.
